questions.question: Which pseudo-element is used to insert generated content before an element's actual content?
questions.explanation: The ::before pseudo-element creates a child at the very beginning of the selected element, and is commonly used with the content property to insert decorative or informational content.

questions.question: What CSS property is required for ::before and ::after pseudo-elements to appear?
questions.explanation: The content property is required for ::before and ::after to render. Without it (even if set to an empty string ''), the pseudo-element will not be generated.

questions.question: Which pseudo-element styles only the first line of text in a block-level element?
questions.explanation: The ::first-line pseudo-element applies styles to the first line of text in a block container. The actual content of the first line depends on the element's width and font size.

questions.question: Which pseudo-element can be used to style the placeholder text of an input field?
questions.explanation: The ::placeholder pseudo-element targets the placeholder text displayed in input and textarea elements before the user enters a value.

questions.question: The CSS ___ property must be set (even to an empty string) for ::before and ::after pseudo-elements to render.
questions.explanation: The content property is mandatory for ::before and ::after. Setting content: '' creates an empty pseudo-element that can still be styled with width, height, background, etc.

questions.question: The _____ pseudo-element allows you to style the portion of text that a user has highlighted.
questions.explanation: The ::selection pseudo-element applies styles to the part of a document that has been highlighted by the user, typically allowing customization of background-color and color.
